payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: prd_review.

Intent:
Layer 0.5 SQLite single-writer dispatcher: leases, heartbeat reaper, admission control, bounded retry, and poison parking.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: run_once dispatcher.py:88, reap_stale_leases :116
heartbeat owner-check state.py:1111 AND leased_by=?
reaper never respawns spawned rows (:142 complete / :152 fail)
admission cap returns backpressure dispatcher.py:91-95
lease/retry columns via forward migrations schema_migrations.py:171-188
poll path routes to dispatcher; _spawn_workflow_job_worker removed (Finding 1)

criteria: public boundaries exist at named symbols
forbidden outcomes are enforceable in code
grill findings resolved with backing code
confidence.rationale: All 5 PRD boundaries verified present in source with correct semantics (owner-checked heartbeat, non-respawning reaper, admission cap, park, forward migrations); grill findings resolved and corroborated by diff; out-of-scope held. Residual is unrun pytest/shasum, acceptable at self_reported grade per handoff.

payload.content: PRD for Layer 0.5 SQLite single-writer dispatcher accepted; all 5 promise contracts map to real public boundaries in current source, grill findings resolved and corroborated by code, out-of-scope respected.

assumptions_to_verify: tests are GREEN at named boundaries when run
artifact sha256 still match handoff values
contradictions_checked: reaper respawning spawned rows (none: completes or fails only)
non-owner heartbeat (blocked by AND leased_by=? clause)
submit rewritten (no: spawn moved from poll driver to dispatcher, submit untouched)
second spawn path (no: private _spawn_workflow_job_worker removed, poll calls dispatcher)
missing_evidence: pytest pass/fail not executed this gate
shasum of PRD/grill artifacts approval-declined (not re-verified against handoff)
critical_review.strongest_objection: Submit-side spawn could have been reintroduced, violating P1 forbidden outcome and Out-of-Scope; or poll could become a second spawn path (Finding 1).
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that submit or poll spawns workers outside WorkflowJobDispatcher, that reap_stale_leases respawns a spawned row, or that heartbeat lacks the owner predicate.

claims: Slice plan covers every PRD promise P1-P5
Each acceptance criterion is backed by a real public boundary in current source

criteria: slices well-formed with PRD-promise + first-RED test
every AC maps to a real source line
confidence.rationale: All 4 slices, their coverage of P1-P5, and all 16 acceptance criteria were verified against current source boundaries and existing named tests; residual is un-run pytest and approval-declined shasum, both self_reported per policy.

payload.content: issues.md defines 4 well-formed AFK slices covering all PRD promises P1-P5; every acceptance criterion maps to a real boundary in the new workflow_job_dispatcher.py, state.py lease APIs, and schema_migrations.py, and each named first-RED test exists verbatim. Out-of-scope (Postgres/SKIP_LOCKED) held; submit unchanged. ACCEPT.

assumptions_to_verify: The provided pytest receipts really came from the current worktree after all relevant files were modified.

P5 bounded retry means both capped delay and max-attempt parking, consistent with ISS-3 acceptance criteria.
contradictions_checked: TDD plan says eight named test cases -> all eight exist in tests/test_dual_agent_workflow_driver.py.
TDD RED/GREEN says CLI/worker heartbeat tests -> no tests reference WorkflowJobLeaseHeartbeat, workflow-job-heartbeat, dispatcher main, --once, or the console script.
ISS-3 says repeated retryable failures park at attempt cap -> no tests reference max_dispatch_attempts_exceeded or perform repeated retry attempts.
Claude changed_files excludes files that git status/diff show as modified and relevant to dispatcher admission/heartbeat wiring.
TDD grill findings 1-3 have partial coverage: backpressure, poison parking, and state-level heartbeat ownership are asserted.
missing_evidence: A test that drives repeated retryable spawn failures until max_dispatch_attempts and asserts status=parked with max_dispatch_attempts_exceeded.
A test for WorkflowJobLeaseHeartbeat or workflow CLI main proving the detached worker starts/stops heartbeat against the spawned job lease.
A test for dispatcher CLI/--once or run_forever entrypoint, or a justified narrowing of ISS-4 acceptance criteria.
An updated changed_files list that includes the MCP poll bridge, workflow CLI heartbeat hook, and pyproject console script.
A live pytest rerun observed by this reviewer or attached logs tying the receipts to the current worktree.
critical_review.strongest_objection: Bounded retry is an explicit task/ISS-3 requirement, but the TDD plan and tests only assert the first retry schedule; there is no public-boundary test proving repeated retryable failures park at the configured max_dispatch_attempts cap.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Add the missing public-boundary tests for max-attempt retry parking and worker/CLI heartbeat or explicitly revise the TDD/issue scope to exclude them, then provide updated passing focused/full test receipts and corrected changed_files evidence.

contradictions_checked: TDD plan says eight named test cases -> all eight exist in tests/test_dual_agent_workflow_driver.py.

ISS-3 says repeated retryable failures park at attempt cap -> no tests reference max_dispatch_attempts_exceeded or perform repeated retry attempts.
Claude changed_files excludes files that git status/diff show as modified and relevant to dispatcher admission/heartbeat wiring.

missing_evidence: A test that drives repeated retryable spawn failures until max_dispatch_attempts and asserts status=parked with max_dispatch_attempts_exceeded.

critical_review.strongest_objection: Bounded retry is an explicit task/ISS-3 requirement, but the TDD plan and tests only assert the first retry schedule; there is no public-boundary test proving repeated retryable failures park at the configured max_dispatch_attempts cap.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Add the missing public-boundary tests for max-attempt retry parking and worker/CLI heartbeat or explicitly revise the TDD/issue scope to exclude them, then provide updated passing focused/full test receipts and corrected changed_files evidence.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Layer 0.5 SQLite single-writer dispatcher: leases, heartbeat reaper, admission control, bounded retry, and poison parking.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
independent_reviewer_non_accept: independent-reviewer-1

claims: 8 core tests cover all P0 safety invariants non-vacuously at the public boundary.
Grill findings 1-3 (backpressure-before-spawn, non-terminal-non-dispatchable park, compare-and-set heartbeat) are resolved and test-backed.
ISS-4's first public-boundary RED test (state heartbeat compare-and-set) is satisfied; its loop/thread deliverables are not.
Tests are currently GREEN against existing implementation (RED-first ordering not independently verifiable from artifacts).

criteria: Verified 8 named tests exist verbatim and are non-vacuous (read :1831-2099).
Verified via grep that run_forever/main/WorkflowJobLeaseHeartbeat are untested across tests/.
Confirmed tdd.md:81 promises CLI/worker-heartbeat tests absent from Test Cases section.
Mapped ISS-4 acceptance criteria (issues.md:67-71) to missing coverage.
confidence.rationale: Coverage gap is verified directly: grep confirms no test references the CLI loop or heartbeat thread, and the plan's own RED/GREEN section promises tests it does not enumerate. The core-invariant coverage is strong and confirmed by reading the 8 tests. Remaining uncertainty is judgment over whether P1 thin-wrapper coverage should block; the independent reviewer non-accept tips this to revise.

Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.

Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

assumptions_to_verify: The pytest receipts were produced from the current dirty worktree revision.
The project owners consider ISS-4 long-lived loop behavior in scope for this TDD gate, not satisfied by --once wiring alone.
The intended P5 max_backoff_s semantics allow or disallow jitter above the cap.
contradictions_checked: TDD plan lists 13 workflow-driver cases; source contains those 13 tests plus the actual schema migration test.
Claude's schema test node lease_migration_v6 is mislabeled; the migration coverage exists under test_forward_migration_adds_workflow_job_dispatcher_leases.
Implementation-plan evidence counts are stale at 9/128/715, while later receipts claim 16/133/720 and source reflects expanded coverage.
Poll compatibility does route through WorkflowJobDispatcher; this part of Claude's claim is supported.
missing_evidence: A public-boundary test that run_forever calls reaper and dispatch repeatedly until a stop_event, or that CLI without --once calls run_forever with the configured interval.
A retry test proving max_backoff_s is enforced when exponential delay exceeds the cap, including the intended jitter semantics.
Independent pytest rerun logs or immutable receipts tied to this exact worktree revision.
Correction of the typed tests array to name the actual schema migration pytest node.
critical_review.strongest_objection: Claude says the earlier CLI-loop coverage gap is closed by test :2274, but that test only invokes main with --once and checks one-tick JSON wiring. Direct search finds no test for WorkflowJobDispatcher.run_forever or CLI execution without --once, despite ISS-4 requiring that the dispatcher loop can run as a long-lived process.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Add or identify tests covering the long-lived dispatcher loop/no---once CLI path and max-backoff cap behavior, or explicitly amend ISS-4/P5 scope to exclude those behaviors, then rerun the focused and full test receipts.

assumptions_to_verify: The pytest receipts were produced from the current dirty worktree revision.
The project owners consider ISS-4 long-lived loop behavior in scope for this TDD gate, not satisfied by --once wiring alone.
The intended P5 max_backoff_s semantics allow or disallow jitter above the cap.
contradictions_checked: TDD plan lists 13 workflow-driver cases; source contains those 13 tests plus the actual schema migration test.
Claude's schema test node lease_migration_v6 is mislabeled; the migration coverage exists under test_forward_migration_adds_workflow_job_dispatcher_leases.
Implementation-plan evidence counts are stale at 9/128/715, while later receipts claim 16/133/720 and source reflects expanded coverage.
Poll compatibility does route through WorkflowJobDispatcher; this part of Claude's claim is supported.
missing_evidence: A public-boundary test that run_forever calls reaper and dispatch repeatedly until a stop_event, or that CLI without --once calls run_forever with the configured interval.
A retry test proving max_backoff_s is enforced when exponential delay exceeds the cap, including the intended jitter semantics.
Independent pytest rerun logs or immutable receipts tied to this exact worktree revision.
Correction of the typed tests array to name the actual schema migration pytest node.
critical_review.strongest_objection: Claude says the earlier CLI-loop coverage gap is closed by test :2274, but that test only invokes main with --once and checks one-tick JSON wiring. Direct search finds no test for WorkflowJobDispatcher.run_forever or CLI execution without --once, despite ISS-4 requiring that the dispatcher loop can run as a long-lived process.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Add or identify tests covering the long-lived dispatcher loop/no---once CLI path and max-backoff cap behavior, or explicitly amend ISS-4/P5 scope to exclude those behaviors, then rerun the focused and full test receipts.

contradictions_checked: TDD plan lists 13 workflow-driver cases; source contains those 13 tests plus the actual schema migration test.

Poll compatibility does route through WorkflowJobDispatcher; this part of Claude's claim is supported.
missing_evidence: A public-boundary test that run_forever calls reaper and dispatch repeatedly until a stop_event, or that CLI without --once calls run_forever with the configured interval.
A retry test proving max_backoff_s is enforced when exponential delay exceeds the cap, including the intended jitter semantics.
Independent pytest rerun logs or immutable receipts tied to this exact worktree revision.
Correction of the typed tests array to name the actual schema migration pytest node.

critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Add or identify tests covering the long-lived dispatcher loop/no---once CLI path and max-backoff cap behavior, or explicitly amend ISS-4/P5 scope to exclude those behaviors, then rerun the focused and full test receipts.

critical_review.strongest_objection: The admission-control proof is single-dispatcher scoped: run_once counts active leases before claiming, and I found no evidence for transactional cap enforcement across multiple dispatcher or poller instances. This is acceptable only because the PRD explicitly scopes Layer 0.5 to one SQLite dispatcher and treats poll as a compatibility bridge.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A live pytest failure, a missing traceability test or source symbol, a requirement that multiple pollers must enforce the admission cap transactionally, or evidence that task docs contain unintended extra artifacts would change this to revise.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Layer 0.5 SQLite single-writer dispatcher: leases, heartbeat reaper, admission control, bounded retry, and poison parking.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: Single-writer claim via _write_lock+BEGIN IMMEDIATE prevents double-claim
Reaper never respawns spawned rows (completes from result or _fail_spawned)
Heartbeat compare-and-set on leased_by prevents wrong-worker lease extension
Admission cap returns backpressure and leaves waiting job reserved
Bounded retry caps backoff and parks at max attempts; poison/malformed payload parks without retry
Migration v6 forward-only, idempotent, non-vacuous

criteria: File ownership exact vs implplan declarations
All traceability tests exist verbatim and are non-vacuous
Dispatcher/state symbols realize the five intent capabilities
Poll bridge routes through dispatcher with no NameError
confidence.rationale: All intent invariants realized at current source and covered by 17 non-vacuous public-boundary tests at exact cited lines; file ownership exact and diff --check clean. Confidence below 0.95 only because pytest/compileall/shasum were not re-run this gate (self_reported per handoff policy).

payload.content: Execution gate ACCEPT. Layer 0.5 SQLite single-writer dispatcher fully realized at current source: leases (claim BEGIN IMMEDIATE single-writer state.py:996), heartbeat reaper (reap:116 never respawns spawned, compare-and-set heartbeat:1110), admission control (run_once cap->backpressure:90), bounded retry (capped backoff+jitter+max-attempt park:250), poison parking (_park malformed/missing payload). File ownership EXACT (10 declared==7 tracked+2 untracked), diff 1020/23 --check clean, migration v6 non-vacuous (6 cols+dispatchable partial idx), poll routed through dispatcher (stdio:2168), 17 traceability tests verbatim+non-vacuous.

assumptions_to_verify: Full suite still 723 passed on a fresh run
No concurrent-poller correctness gap beyond Layer 0.5 single-writer scope
contradictions_checked: Reaper respawning spawned rows (refuted: :130-153 completes-or-fails, never re-Popen)
Heartbeat allowing foreign worker to extend lease (refuted: WHERE leased_by=? rowcount==1 :1110-1117)
Poll becoming a second spawn implementation (refuted: stdio:2168 delegates to dispatcher, inline _write/_spawn removed from poll path)
Undeclared file changes (refuted: all git changes map to declared paths)
poll bridge NameError on os/_pid_alive (refuted: os:13, _pid_alive:4732 in stdio)
missing_evidence: Live pytest run output for the 17 tests + full suite
compileall exit status re-verified
planning-artifact sha256 re-hash vs handoff
critical_review.strongest_objection: Headline pass evidence (723 passed, compileall, shasum) is implementer self-reported and was not re-executed during this gate, so GREEN is asserted not observed.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A failing/erroring test run, a respawn of a spawned row in the reaper, a claim path that double-leases under the write lock, or an undeclared file in the diff.

contradictions_checked: Claude test_status unknown vs Cursor execution: 155 pytest passed here
handoff SHA256 vs disk: prd/issues/tdd/grill-findings/implementation-plan all match outcome-review.md
PRD no SKIP LOCKED vs impl BEGIN IMMEDIATE single-writer: consistent
PRD submit unchanged vs diff: submit reserves; poll delegates to WorkflowJobDispatcher
grill spawned respawn ban vs reap_stale_leases: fails/completes spawned rows, never respawns
legacy _spawn_workflow_job_worker exists but poll calls _drive_dual_agent_workflow_job only
missing_evidence: full-suite pytest -q (723) re-execution in this gate

reaper test for expired lease on alive spawned worker (impl handles it; only dead-pid path tested)
critical_review.strongest_objection: Poll bridge, CLI, and run_forever tests use FakeDispatcher/monkeypatched stubs, so production poll→real dispatcher→spawn is not integration-proven despite production code wiring.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Any failing dispatcher/lease/migration pytest, planning SHA mismatch, or observed duplicate spawn / second spawn path bypassing pid IS NULL guard.

assumptions_to_verify: Only one active dispatcher service is intended for admission-cap correctness
Receipt-reported full suite remains green in a writable test environment
Initial heartbeat rejection is not required to abort the worker immediately
contradictions_checked: No Postgres or SKIP LOCKED implementation found; source uses SQLite BEGIN IMMEDIATE claim path
Submit-side spawn claim checked: submit reserves only and tests assert no Popen before poll
Poll second-spawn implementation checked: poll delegates to WorkflowJobDispatcher, though old private helpers remain unreachable
Duplicate same-job spawn checked: claim filters pid IS NULL and concurrent same-job poll test asserts one Popen
Non-owner heartbeat checked: state update requires leased_by match and rowcount==1
Planning artifact hashes checked: five source artifact shasums matched handoff values
missing_evidence: Fresh pytest and compileall execution in this read-only review environment
Operational evidence for a supervised long-lived dispatcher under real scheduling/load
Explicit test for initial worker heartbeat rejection behavior
critical_review.strongest_objection: Admission cap is not transactionally proven across multiple dispatcher or poller instances; this is acceptable only because the PRD scopes Layer 0.5 to a single SQLite dispatcher and poll compatibility, while same-job duplicate spawn is guarded.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A failing dispatcher or migration test, evidence of reachable spawn outside WorkflowJobDispatcher, a requirement for multi-dispatcher transactional admission enforcement, or a planning artifact hash mismatch would change this to revise.
